# Pin every documented exit code.

# 0 — success.
exec eon add --cron '@hourly' -- echo hi
exec eon ls
exec eon status
exec eon stop

# 2 — usage. Cover application-level (errUsage) AND cobra-level
# (UnknownCommand / UnknownFlag / ExactArgs / flag parse) variants.
# The "Usage error:" prefix is emitted by exitCode==2 paths only,
# so checking for it pins the mapping.
! exec eon add
! exec eon ls --kind weird
! exec eon show abc

# Unknown command / unknown flag → usage.
! exec eon foo
stderr '[Uu]nknown command'
! exec eon --badflag
stderr 'Usage error.*unknown flag'

# Wrong arg count → usage. ExactArgs / RangeArgs / NoArgs violations.
! exec eon show
stderr 'Usage error.*accepts 1 arg'
! exec eon show 1 extra
stderr 'Usage error.*accepts 1 arg'
! exec eon rm
! exec eon enable
! exec eon disable
! exec eon logs
! exec eon ls extra
! exec eon status extra
! exec eon stop extra
# edit was removed — invocation hits the "unknown command" path.
! exec eon edit something --cron '@hourly'
stderr '[Uu]nknown command'

# Cobra-level flag parse errors → usage.
! exec eon logs 1 --since garbage
stderr 'Usage error.*invalid argument'
! exec eon show -1
stderr 'Usage error.*shorthand'

# 3 — not found.
! exec eon show 9999
! exec eon rm 9999
! exec eon enable 9999
! exec eon disable 9999
! exec eon logs 9999

# 5 — precondition failed (invalid input that's syntactically reachable).
! exec eon add --cron 'garbage' -- echo
! exec eon add --at 'yesterday' -- echo
! exec eon add --at '1970-01-01T00:00:00Z' -- echo
# @every with non-positive duration would tight-loop the scheduler.
! exec eon add --cron '@every 0s' -- echo
stderr 'invalid cron.*positive'
! exec eon add --cron '@every -1s' -- echo
stderr 'invalid cron.*positive'

# logs flag-value sanity (negative values must be rejected, not
# silently mis-interpreted).
exec eon add --cron '@hourly' --name logreg -- /bin/echo hi
! exec eon logs logreg --lines -1
stderr 'Usage error.*--lines'
! exec eon logs logreg --since -1m
stderr 'Usage error.*--since'
